purpose: Review source-attested, structurally valid syllables missing from the current materialized inventory. This file does not assign Yinyuan IDs, codes, or keys.

de4.decision_basis: Wanxiang contains repeated colloquial phrase instances such as еҳҡз‘ҹ; admission is restricted to multi-character source records.

kuai2.decision_basis: The local merged Unihan material records U+351E г”һ as kuГ i and Wanxiang supplies the source-attested kuГЎi reading; the review preserves the attested form rather than guessing a replacement.

lan5.decision_basis: Wanxiang preserves an isolated и—Қ lan source row, while word-level evidence places the unmarked syllable in contexts such as ж …ж Ҹ and the жұүе…ё иӢӨи—Қ page. Preserve it as word-context pronunciation evidence without creating a standalone neutral-tone character candidate.

sa2.decision_basis: Wanxiang supplies repeated regional food-name records using sГЎ; local Unihan-derived material retains other readings, so this is recorded as a source-attested regional variant, not inferred from them.
